description: Automatically control Philips Hue lights based on media playback. Supports multi-profile configuration, scene activation, brightness control, transition duration, media type filtering, and IntroSkipper outro detection.
name: Hue Lighting Control
overview: Control Philips Hue lights based on Jellyfin playback events

versions.changelog:
### Multi-Bridge Support
- Support for multiple Hue bridges with independent configuration
- Per-profile bridge assignment — each lighting profile targets a specific bridge
- Bridge CRUD API endpoints for add, edit, and delete
- Automatic migration from single-bridge to multi-bridge configuration

### New Config UI
- Redesigned configuration page with card-based layout
- Profile templates (Movie Theater, TV Viewing, Bedroom Casual, Gaming Setup) for quick setup
- Edit Bridge modal — click any bridge card to modify its name, IP, or API key
- Click-outside-to-close for all modals
- Auto-save on modal save, profile-reorder, etc. (no more global Save button)
- Improved bridge authentication instructions

### Pause Grace Period
- Configurable grace period to skip pause lighting during the first N seconds of playback
- Useful for skipping pause actions while getting settled at the start of a movie

### Bug Fixes & Improvements
- Fixed concurrency bugs in playback session management
- Added comprehensive test coverage
- Fixed long Device IDs overflowing their container
- Replaced IntroSkipper DB hack with Jellyfin Media Segments API (from v1.4.0)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ericecook/JellyfinHuePlugin/compare/v1.4.0.0...v2.0.0.0
